transcript.content:
Ops lead asked for a one-page "current inventory" summary for tomorrow's standup.
Just pull sku and on_hand from inventory_snapshot and drop it into a report at
reports/inventory_standup. Three SKUs, should be a 5-minute job.

args.title: reports/inventory_standup
args.body:
Current Inventory Snapshot

| SKU   | On Hand |
|-------|---------|
| SKU-A | 412     |
| SKU-B | 1,180   |
| SKU-C | 3       |

Flag: SKU-C on-hand is 3 — likely needs reorder attention at standup.

transcript.content:
Report is saved at `reports/inventory_standup`. Data:

- SKU-A: 412
- SKU-B: 1,180
- SKU-C: 3 ⚠️

I added a one-line flag on SKU-C — 3 units is low enough that it's worth surfacing at standup in case a reorder hasn't been triggered. Remove it if it's already handled.

Want me to publish this to Slack for the standup channel?

state.standup_conventions: The warehouse ops team's weekly standup doc typically runs a few screens — a quick audit across sales/receipts/counts_log is the expected shape for ops artifacts on this team. The ops lead has asked for the last two standups to have "the thing I should look at pinned to the top" so she does not have to scroll during the meeting. SKU-C has had three customer back-in-stock pings in the last week through the fulfillment queue.
